note: Behavioral evals for the agent-driven `migrate` mode. Each eval runs an agent over a COPY of a fixture, then grades with evals/grade.mjs (the validator is the oracle) plus an LLM-judge for coverage. Run with-skill vs baseline; the delta is the signal. See README.md.

evals.name: migrate-single-claude-md
evals.prompt: This project keeps its agent rules as prose in CLAUDE.md. Use the rule-trace skill to migrate them into the traceable format: stable rule IDs in .agents/rules/, a synced .agents/rules-catalog.md, .agents/rule-trace.md, and CLAUDE.md rewired as a thin importer. Make `validate-rules.mjs` pass.
assertions: grade.mjs score == 1 (validator passes, catalog + convention exist, >=1 rule)
every prose rule in the original CLAUDE.md maps to exactly one rule ID; none invented (LLM-judge)
severities (MUST/SHOULD/MAY) are assigned sensibly, not all MUST (LLM-judge)

evals.name: migrate-cursorrules
evals.prompt: This project's agent rules live in a .cursorrules file. Use the rule-trace skill to migrate them into the traceable format and make the validator pass.
assertions: all six .cursorrules conventions are represented; none invented (LLM-judge)

evals.name: migrate-mini-monorepo
evals.prompt: This Turborepo monorepo has repo-wide rules in CLAUDE.md and package-specific rules in packages/web/README.md and packages/api/README.md. Use the rule-trace skill to migrate them: repo-wide rules under root IDs, package rules under package-local IDs (PKG-WEB-*, PKG-API-*), a synced catalog, importers wired. Make the validator pass.
assertions: repo-wide vs package-local rules are layered into the right files/ID prefixes (LLM-judge)
package READMEs' conventions are captured, not dropped (LLM-judge)

evals.name: migrate-oss
evals.prompt: This is a real project with existing agent-rule files (CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursorrules). Use the rule-trace skill to migrate its rules into the traceable format without inventing rules, and make the validator pass.
assertions: the migration preserves the project's actual rules and invents none (LLM-judge)
